description: Schema for the FEM simulation input file

boundary_conditions_parameters.description: Defines how the domain is restrained — which nodes are fixed, in which directions, and how those nodes are identified.

constrain_directions.description: Which displacement components to lock at the constrained nodes. Use 'x' or 'y' to fix a single axis, or 'xy' to fully pin the node. When provided as a list, each entry maps to the corresponding group of constrained DOFs.

constrained_degrees_of_freedom.description: Global DOF indices to be fixed (zero-displacement Dirichlet conditions). Only used when dof_selection_mode is 'explicit' or 'hybrid'.

dof_selection_mode.description: How constrained DOFs are identified. 'explicit' uses the manually listed indices in constrained_degrees_of_freedom. 'window' selects DOFs automatically based on the window_value_threshold. 'hybrid' combines both — manual indices plus any DOFs that pass the window filter.

window_value_threshold.description: Normalised coordinate threshold (0–1) used in 'window' or 'hybrid' mode to automatically select boundary DOFs. Nodes whose normalised position falls below this value are constrained.

forcing_parameters.description: Specifies the external loading applied to the system. Exactly one mode is active per run; the sub-objects for the other modes are still required but ignored.

body_parameters.description: Uniform volumetric load applied to every element (e.g. gravity or self-weight). Active when mode is 'body'.

magnitude_x.description: Body-force intensity in the x-direction (force per unit volume).

magnitude_y.description: Body-force intensity in the y-direction (force per unit volume).

concentrated_parameters.description: Point loads applied directly to specific DOFs. Active when mode is 'concentrated'.

forced_degrees_of_freedom.description: Global DOF indices where the point load is applied. Each listed DOF receives the same magnitude. If left empty, defaults to target_dof.

magnitude.description: Force value applied at each listed DOF. The sign determines direction (positive follows the positive axis).

mode.description: Selects which forcing strategy is active for the run. 'concentrated' applies point loads at specific DOFs, 'window' distributes a load across DOFs selected by a spatial filter, and 'body' applies a uniform volumetric force everywhere. 'concentrated' with an empty forced_degrees_of_freedom list defaults to applying the load at target_dof.

window_parameters.description: Spatially filtered distributed load — force is applied to every DOF whose normalised position exceeds the filter threshold. Active when mode is 'window'.

filter_threshold.description: Normalised coordinate cutoff (0–1). DOFs with a normalised position above this value receive the load; those below are unloaded.

magnitude.description: Force value applied at each DOF that passes the window filter.

lagrange_multiplier_parameters.description: Controls an optional set of constraints enforced via Lagrange multipliers. This adds extra rows/columns to the system matrix to enforce exact constraint satisfaction.

bound_degrees_of_freedom.description: Global DOF indices to constrain via Lagrange multipliers. Only used when dof_selection_mode is 'explicit' or 'hybrid'.

constrain_directions.description: Which displacement components the Lagrange multiplier constraints act on ('x', 'y', or 'xy' for both).

dof_selection_mode.description: How constrained DOFs are chosen. 'explicit' uses bound_degrees_of_freedom directly, 'window' selects by spatial threshold, 'hybrid' combines both.

enabled.description: If true, the Lagrange multiplier constraints are assembled and enforced. Set to false to disable without removing the configuration.

window_value_threshold.description: Normalised coordinate threshold (0–1) for automatic DOF selection in 'window' or 'hybrid' mode.

loop_parameters.description: Controls the quasi-static loading loop and the Newton-Raphson nonlinear solver used at each increment.

newton_raphson_maximum_steps.description: Upper limit on Newton-Raphson iterations per timestep. If convergence is not reached within this many iterations, the step is considered failed.

newton_raphson_tolerance.description: Residual norm threshold below which the Newton-Raphson solver is considered converged. Smaller values give tighter equilibrium but cost more iterations.

timesteps.description: Total number of quasi-static load increments. The full load or displacement is divided evenly across this many steps.

n_threads_petsc.description: Number of MPI/OpenMP processes to launch when the PETSc solver is selected. MPI/OpenMP count is determined automatically based on the CPU architecture. Ignored if sparse_solver is 'scipy'.

optimise_sparsity.description: If true, the assembler pre-computes and caches the sparsity pattern of the global stiffness matrix, avoiding repeated memory allocations. Recommended for large meshes; the up-front cost is repaid over many timesteps.

output_parameters.description: Controls which result files are written during and after the simulation.

pvd_gif_parameters.description: Post-processing step that renders the saved PVD files into an animated GIF for quick visual inspection.

axis_limits.description: Fixed axis bounds for every frame ([x/y_min, x/y_max]).

distortion_scaling.description: Multiplier applied to the displacement field before rendering. Values greater than 1 exaggerate deformations for visibility; 1.0 shows true scale.

enabled.description: If true, a GIF is generated from the PVD time series after the simulation finishes.

fps.description: Playback speed of the output GIF in frames per second.

include_every_n.description: Frame-skipping factor. A value of 1 includes every timestep; 2 includes every second timestep, etc. Useful for keeping file size down on long runs.

mesh_outline.description: If true, element edges are drawn over the contour plot so the mesh topology is visible.

target_fields.description: Names of the nodal/elemental fields to visualise (e.g. 'displacement', 'phasefield'). One GIF is produced per field.

save_logs.description: If true, stream the output into a log file. Otherwise, output is printed to the consoled and not saved.

save_pickle.description: If true, all of the computed fields are saved to a pickle file.

save_pvd.description: If true, ParaView-compatible PVD/VTU files are written at each timestep for visualisation and post-processing.

stream_outputs.description: If true, post-processed quantities of interest are flushed to files incrementally during the run, rather than only at the end. Useful for long simulations or when memory is limited.

timestamp.description: If true, output directories and filenames include a timestamp to avoid overwriting results from previous runs.

parent_input_file.description: Path to another input JSON that this file inherits from.

penalty_method_parameters.description: Controls an optional set of constraints enforced via the penalty method. A large stiffness is added to penalised DOFs, approximately (not exactly) enforcing the constraint. Simpler than Lagrange multipliers but introduces a tuneable penalty_value.

bound_degrees_of_freedom.description: Global DOF indices to penalise. Only used when dof_selection_mode is 'explicit' or 'hybrid'.

constrain_directions.description: Which displacement components the penalty acts on ('x', 'y', or 'xy' for both).

dof_selection_mode.description: How penalised DOFs are chosen. 'explicit' uses bound_degrees_of_freedom directly, 'window' selects by spatial threshold, 'hybrid' combines both.

enabled.description: If true, penalty terms are added to the global stiffness matrix. Set to false to disable without removing the configuration.

penalty_value.description: Stiffness multiplier added to penalised DOFs. Must be large enough relative to the physical stiffness to suppress motion, but excessively large values cause ill-conditioning.

window_value_threshold.description: Normalised coordinate threshold (0–1) for automatic DOF selection in 'window' or 'hybrid' mode.

solution_parameters.description: Chooses between load-controlled and displacement-controlled solution strategies and configures each.

displacement_control_parameters.description: Settings for displacement control, where a prescribed displacement is ramped up over the load steps. Useful for capturing post-peak softening behaviour that load control cannot follow.

loading_directions.description: The direction(s) in which the displacement is applied. I.e. if the final value is set to 5e-3, [1, -0.5] will load to 5e-3 and then to -2.5e-3. Useful for cyclic loading.

maximum_controlled_displacement.description: Maximum prescribed displacement to reach by the final timestep. The increment per step is this value divided by the number of timesteps, unless more tricky loading directions are specified.

load_control_parameters.description: Settings for load control, where the external force is ramped linearly from zero to its full value over the load steps.

loading_directions.description: Analogous to the loading_directions in displacement control, but for forces.

mode.description: Active solution strategy. 'load control' ramps external forces and solves for displacements; 'displacement control' prescribes displacements and solves for the required reaction forces.

sparse_solver.description: Which linear algebra backend solves the global system. 'scipy' uses SciPy's built-in sparse direct solver (single-threaded, no extra dependencies). 'petsc' uses PETSc, which supports parallel solves via n_threads_petsc.
